cases.description: Packaging read-only optioning should stay in the packaging family.
cases.userRequest: I have a 54-card poker deck and a short rules leaflet. Which TGC packaging options should I compare first, and what packaging art or measurements would you need from me before setup?

cases.description: Deck read-only optioning should stay in the card/deck family.
cases.userRequest: I'm making a 54-card poker-size deck. Should I use shared backs or unique backs, and what else would you want to know before setting up the deck?

cases.description: Board and mat planning should stay in the board/mat family.
cases.userRequest: I need a foldable central board that players open on the table. What board or mat options should I compare, and what proofing risks matter most before I choose one?

cases.description: Custom-cut planning should stay in the custom-cut family.
cases.userRequest: I want punchout standees with bases and a rotating dial. What custom-cut workflows apply, and what cut-line or geometry issues should I plan for?

cases.description: Book and score pad planning should stay in the book/rulebook family.
cases.userRequest: I need a rules booklet and maybe a score pad. Which document or book components should I compare, and what page or file constraints matter most?

cases.description: Printed parts planning should stay in the parts/dice family.
cases.userRequest: I want custom printed dice and meeples in the same game. What should I know before setting them up, and what art or component constraints matter first?

cases.description: A mixed component planning question should stay in the router/orchestration family.
cases.userRequest: I'm building a prototype with a 54-card deck, punchout tokens, a folded board, and a box. Help me choose the component plan and the order I should set things up.
